title: Today's Tech Brief: Oura's New Ring 5 Is Smaller and Lighter—and Adds an AI Health Coach
summary: A look at Oura's new Ring 5, AI sticker shock in corporate America, and more tech news.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Oura’s New Ring 5 Is Smaller and Lighter—and Adds an AI Health Coach
imageCaption: Oura’s New Ring 5 Is Smaller and Lighter—and Adds an AI Health Coach
imageSourceTitle: Oura’s New Ring 5 Is Smaller and Lighter—and Adds an AI Health Coach

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's tech brief. Finnish health tech company Oura just launched its new smart ring, the Oura Ring 5. It's smaller, lighter, and adds an AI health coach to the mix. This comes two years after the Oura Ring 4 and right in time to compete with other screenless fitness trackers like the Fitbit Air. The new ring seems like a solid upgrade, with its sleek design and AI-powered health features making health tracking more accessible and user-friendly.

I'm intrigued by the potential for this tech to integrate with other health apps and services, which could make it a game-changer for those looking to track their well-being. For instance, imagine being able to seamlessly connect your Oura Ring 5 to your favorite meditation or fitness app. It's definitely worth keeping an eye on.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
